title: Microsoft IIS HTTP.sys Request Queue Length per Application Pool
dataSources: `index=windows` IIS HTTP.sys Perfmon counters (`HTTP Service Request Queue\CurrentQueueSize`) via `Splunk_TA_windows` Perfmon or `Nmon`
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`); Splunk Add-on for Microsoft IIS (Splunkbase 3185)
spl:
index=windows sourcetype="Perfmon:IIS" OR sourcetype="perfmon"
| search object="HTTP Service Request Queue" OR counter="CurrentQueueSize"
| eval qsize=tonumber(Value)
| where qsize > 100
| timechart span=1m max(qsize) by instance
description: HTTP.sys queues requests before IIS worker threads dequeue them. Queue growth often appears before application-level latency and implicates kernel-layer throttling, thread starvation, or SYN floods.
value: Explains latency that application profilers miss and speeds isolation between network, kernel, and code paths.
implementation: Collect Perfmon every 60s; map `instance` to site/app pool. Alert when queue sustained above baseline.
visualization: Stacked bars for status/substatus, Perfmon timecharts, top client tables.
grandmaExplanation: We watch HTTP.sys queues so IIS does not drop connections before your code runs.
